13.name: Graphics Engine Exception
13.likely_cause: Out-of-bounds array access, illegal instruction, or illegal register access within a kernel. Rarely a hardware fault.
13.notes: Run under compute-sanitizer (memcheck) or cuda-gdb to isolate the offending kernel.

14.name: Channel Exception
14.likely_cause: Generic channel-level error, often tied to command submission corruption.
14.notes: One of the most commonly cited example Xids in NVIDIA's own docs.

31.name: GPU Memory Page Fault
31.likely_cause: Use-after-free, allocator race condition (e.g. competing memory pools across PyTorch/CuPy/TensorRT), or DLPack zero-copy tensor lifetime bug.
31.notes: Most common 'CUDA process crashed' Xid. Message includes faulting address, engine (GRAPHICS/COPY/NVDEC), fault type (PDE/PTE), and access type.

43.name: GPU Stopped Processing
43.likely_cause: Driver or application-triggered stop, sometimes preceding a more specific error.
43.notes: Often appears alongside other Xids as a secondary symptom.

45.name: Preemptive Channel Removal
45.likely_cause: Driver reclaimed a channel, frequently after another fault or a killed process.
45.notes: Usually informational/secondary rather than root cause.

48.name: Double Bit ECC Error (DBE)
48.likely_cause: Uncorrectable DRAM cell degradation. Two bits flipped in the same memory word; ECC can detect but not correct.
48.notes: Corrupts any data in that location (e.g. training gradients). Can cascade into Xid 31 if it hits page tables. If recurring, RMA the GPU.

54.name: Power Cable Issue
54.likely_cause: Auxiliary power cable unplugged or seated poorly.
54.notes: Visible directly via nvidia-smi power-cable status field.

63.name: ECC Row Remapping Pending
63.likely_cause: A memory row has accumulated enough single-bit errors to require remapping; remap is pending a GPU reset.
63.notes: Introduced with A100+ row-remap error recovery, replacing older dynamic page retirement.

64.name: ECC Row Remapping Failure
64.likely_cause: Row remap could not be applied successfully, indicating deeper memory degradation.
64.notes: Escalate directly; this is a stronger signal than Xid 63.

74.name: NVLink Error
74.likely_cause: Link-level failure between GPUs or GPU-to-NVSwitch, e.g. training a multi-GPU job with degraded interconnect.
74.notes: Cluster-level issue — investigate the fabric/switch, not just the single reporting GPU.

79.name: GPU Has Fallen Off the Bus
79.likely_cause: GPU became unresponsive on the PCIe bus, often due to power, thermal, seating, or hardware failure.
79.notes: Frequently requires a physical reseat or node reboot if reset doesn't recover it. Can also appear as multiline log entries without an explicit Xid tag.

95.name: Uncontained Error / Multi-App Containment Failure
95.likely_cause: An error (often ECC-related) affected multiple applications sharing the GPU and was not successfully contained.
95.notes: Affected GPU must be reset before any new applications can run on it.

119.name: GSP RPC Timeout
119.likely_cause: The GPU System Processor (GSP) microcontroller failed to respond to a driver RPC call in time.
119.notes: Uncommon outside of hardware failure. If persistent, revert recent hardware changes and cold-reset the system.

120.name: GSP Error
120.likely_cause: An error occurred in code running on the GPU's GSP core.
120.notes: Often paired with Xid 119 in logs.

144.name: NVLink5 Fatal Link Event
144.likely_cause: Fatal-severity NVLink5 fabric error (e.g. NETIR_LINK_EVT subcode) on newer NVLink5-generation interconnects.
144.notes: New error family (Xids 144-150) for NVLink5 hardware. Carries extended subcode/severity/link-number fields in the log line — tooling support is still sparse, worth prioritizing.

154.name: GPU Recovery Action Required
154.likely_cause: Driver has determined a specific recovery action is needed for the GPU.
154.notes: Visible via nvidia-smi's GPU Recovery Action field; pair with nvidia-smi output when parsing.

0.name: Unknown / Unmapped Xid
0.likely_cause: Xid code not present in this reference table.
0.notes: Fallback entry. Consult NVIDIA's full Xid catalog (docs.nvidia.com/deploy/xid-errors) or nverror.h in open-gpu-kernel-modules for the definitive list.
